evals.name: concision positive classic model
evals.prompt: Use the oiticica-concision skill. Example type: public-domain source-model paraphrase, not a quotation. <example>A public-domain source model from Lincoln's Gettysburg Address: selected words carry civic argument without surplus explanation</example> Task: name the concept as oiticica-concision. Treat the described source-model relation as strong. Do not infer a fault from the absence of a longer source passage. In Preserve, copy only the literal text inside <example> exactly as supplied. If Preserve adds any source wording, quoted passage, or imagined prose, the answer fails. Do not rewrite it into a Weak/Better contrast. Use the headings Principle, Preserve, Why, and Rubric. Under Rubric, give at least two named objective checks from the skill and mark pass or fail. For repair-only checks, mark Pass when no repair is needed because the model is strong.
evals.expected_output: The response names the skill concept, preserves a strong classic model, and judges it by objective rubric checks.
assertions: The output identifies the relevant Oiticica concept as oiticica-concision or concision.
The output includes a Preserve section or explicitly says the model should be preserved or treated as strong.
The output applies at least two objective rubric checks instead of generic praise.

evals.name: concision negative classic contrast
evals.prompt: Use the oiticica-concision skill. Example type: invented weak passage, not a quotation. Example: The celestial orb of daytime commenced its ascent above the eastern horizon. Task: give one concrete contrast with Weak, Fault, Better, Why, and Rubric. Use the Example text exactly in Weak. In Fault, name the exact broken relation instead of relying on labels such as unclear, awkward, vague, or verbose.
evals.expected_output: The response gives a concrete Oiticica contrast and fixes the named fault.
assertions: The output includes Weak, Fault, Better, Why, and Rubric sections.
The output names a concrete fault in relation, sequence, diction, syntax, sound, or reading rather than saying only unclear, awkward, vague, or verbose.
The Better version repairs the fault while preserving the intended meaning.
